q: What is saltatory conduction and why does it increase the speed of nerve impulses?
options: It is the simultaneous firing of all neurons in a nerve bundle, increasing signal strength
The action potential jumps between nodes of Ranvier on myelinated axons, reducing the distance the impulse must travel continuously
Sodium ions are actively transported along the axon surface to boost signal velocity
It describes the all-or-nothing amplification of weak signals at each axon segment
explanation: In myelinated axons, the insulating myelin sheath forces ionic currents to jump between exposed nodes of Ranvier, drastically increasing conduction speed compared to unmyelinated fibers.
